inputs.label: Symbol 1 (leftmost)
inputs.businessContext: Value of the first Roman numeral symbol (e.g., M=1000, C=100, X=10, I=1). Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 2
inputs.businessContext: Value of the second symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 3
inputs.businessContext: Value of the third symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 4
inputs.businessContext: Value of the fourth symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 5
inputs.businessContext: Value of the fifth symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 6
inputs.businessContext: Value of the sixth symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 7
inputs.businessContext: Value of the seventh symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

inputs.label: Symbol 8 (rightmost)
inputs.businessContext: Value of the eighth symbol. Enter 0 if unused.

formulas.Enter_the_values_of_Roman_numeral_symbol: Enter the values of Roman numeral symbols (I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000) in order from left to right.
formulas.The_calculator_applies_subtractive_notat: The calculator applies subtractive notation: for each pair, if a symbol is smaller than the next, its value is subtracted; otherwise it is added.
formulas.The_final_result_is_the_equivalent_Arabi: The final result is the equivalent Arabic number.

breakdown: Step 1: Enter the values of Roman numeral symbols (I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000) in order from left to right.
Step 2: The calculator applies subtractive notation: for each pair, if a symbol is smaller than the next, its value is subtracted; otherwise it is added.
Step 3: The final result is the equivalent Arabic number.
